quoted_status.full_text:
【重大発表】アイデアソンで見つけた強みを活かして
Claude Codeやエージェントを実務に落とし込む

1Dayアイデアソンを
4/24（金）20時から一夜限定で開催します！

今回のテーマは「アイデアソン5日間のその先」。

5日間で1,000人以上が「自分を知る」を体験した。

個性心理学で強みを言語化して、経験を棚卸しして、
自分だけの武器を見つけて、AIで最大化する。

実はここまでがAI時代の"1.0"。

正直に言うと、ほとんどの人がここで止まってる。

「自分を知った。じゃあ次、どうすんの？」

AI時代は2.0に入った。
チャットでAIに聞く時代から、エージェントが実務を回す時代に変わった。

Claude Code、Skills設計、ワークフロー自動化。
知ってるだけの人と、毎日の実務で回してる人の差が開き続けてる。

じゃあ実際どうやって落とし込むのか。

✅億超えマーケターしか知らないClaude Codeの活用法
✅完全自動で回して3日でフォロワー2,000人増やした仕組み
✅手動の業務を自動化させる思考ワーク

強みを見つけたその先となる道筋を1Dayアイデアソンで公開します。

5日間のアイデアソンが初心者向けだったのに対して、今回は中上級者向け。

エージェント時代にAIを実務で使い倒したい人だけに向けてお伝えします。

5Daysに来てくれた人は「自分を知る」が済んでるから、即実装に入れる。

来れなかった人も問題ない。AI時代2.0の答えが欲しいなら来てほしい。

参加方法は下記の通り。

───────────────

【1Dayアイデアソンへの参加方法】

① でぷれ（@ai_depression）をフォロー
② この投稿にいいね＆【　参加　】とリプ
③ リプ欄のURLから申し込み

※さらに引用リポストをしてくれた方には後日アーカイブもプレゼント

───────────────

それでは、4月24日20時にまたお会いしましょう。

tweets.full_text:
【Claude Codeに投げた言葉】(3/5)

ターミナルで日本語でこう頼んだ。

「マニュアル生成スキルを作ってください。

　入力：テーマ・対象者・内容・注意事項
　出力：HTMLファイルのマニュアル

　デザインの条件：
　・背景は白・見出しはネイビー
　・フォントはゴシック系統一
　・セクションごとに罫線で区切る
　・スマホでも読みやすいレイアウト」

30分後に動くスキルができてた。

コードの中身はわからないけど、
動いてるのは確認できた。

user.name: てんそ｜医療× Claude Code
user.location: 🔻医療職用ChatGPTプロンプト公開中🔻
user.description: 大学病院20年勤務の医療職がClaude Codeで現場業務を自動化実践中｜勤務表アプリ作成で80%効率化を実現｜研修資料・マニュアル作成をAIで半自動化に挑戦中｜生成AIパスポート取得｜AIコーチ・講演実績あり｜医療現場をラクにするAI活用を発信

tweets.full_text:
【スキルって何なのか】(2/5)

Claude Codeのスキルっていうのは、
一度作ったら繰り返し使える
専用の命令セットみたいなもの。

毎回プロンプトを書かなくていい。

今回作ったのはこういうスキル。

「テーマ・対象者・注意事項を入力すると
　決まったデザインのマニュアルが
　HTMLファイルで出力される」

デザインの指定も全部スキルに組み込んだから、
色・フォント・レイアウトが
毎回自動で統一される。

tweets.full_text:
I built a lead enrichment tool in 4 hours using Claude Code that saves me $800/month. Here's the build: 1. Asked Claude to write a Python script that takes a CSV of company names and returns LinkedIn URLs
2. It used Selenium to automate Google searches and scrape the first result
3. Built error handling for rate limits and CAPTCHAs
4. Added a 2-second delay between searches to avoid detection
5. Output writes to a new CSV with original data plus enriched LinkedIn URLs
6. Connected it to Make using a webhook trigger
7. Now I upload CSVs to a Google Drive folder and Make runs the script automatically
8. Results get pushed into a Google Sheet with timestamp and source tracking
9. Mistake 1: forgot to handle non-existent companies, script crashed on blanks
10. Fixed it by adding validation to skip empty rows
11. Mistake 2: LinkedIn blocks too many requests from the same IP
12. Solved by rotating proxies every 50 searches using a paid proxy service
13. Total cost now: $12/month for proxies vs $800/month for a SaaS doing the same thing AI isn't taking your job, it's taking the job of people who won't learn to use it. sharing what I'm building with Claude Code + Make daily. follow if you want to see the builds.

An agentic harness = the scaffolding that turns AI into a doer, not just a talker.

Claude Code and Codex are among the best, and both ship with any paid Claude or ChatGPT plan. Know this: these are not just for writing code.

Launch one in the terminal. Give it skills/instructions, tools, and memory. Watch it handle real business tasks start to finish.

Nantucket professionals: what are you building with Claude Code or Codex?

Tag @nantucketai with your local AI onboarding questions.

tweets.full_text:
Everyone's looking at this as a Claude Code tutorial. The real story is bigger.

What you're watching is the first version of how engineering teams will be structured in 18 months. The org chart stops mattering when a 4-person team with a mature Team OS ships faster than a 40-person team using AI individually.

Think about what's inside a Team OS. Shared skills that encode how your specific codebase works. Shared automations that enforce your team's quality bar without code review bottlenecks. A learning flywheel where every bug fix, every deploy, every PR review makes the system smarter for everyone.

This is the Spotify model, the squad model, the two-pizza team concept, except the constraint that killed all of those was knowledge transfer. Senior engineers left and took half the team's context with them. A Team OS on GitHub means the context stays even when people don't.

The companies running these systems are going to look at hiring completely differently. You stop asking "how many engineers do we need" and start asking "how good is our Team OS." A team of 5 with 50 shared skills will outship a team of 20 with none, every single quarter.

The early movers have maybe a 6 month window before this becomes obvious to everyone. After that, recruiting without a Team OS will feel like recruiting without a tech stack.

tweets.full_text:
i have shared this before and im sharing this again for my new followers

this is a must if you're still writing prompts from scratch like it's 2024..

someone built a claude code skill that scrapes reddit, x, youtube, tiktok, hackernews, polymarket, github and more from the last 30 days on any topic.. 

then writes you copy paste ready prompts based on what real people are actually using right now..

it's called last30days-skill. 23k stars. MIT license. open source

look.. the problem with every prompt library on the internet is they go stale within weeks. 

what worked for chatgpt in january probably doesn't work today. this skill skips that entirely by pulling live data from communities where builders share what's actually working right now

how to set it up

> make sure you have claude code installed
> then run this in your terminal

/plugin marketplace add mvanhorn/last30days-skill

> enable code execution (required for the skill to run its scrapers)
> that's it

now in claude code you just type
/last30days prompting techniques for claude code when refactoring large codebases..

and it goes off. scans reddit threads, x posts, youtube transcripts, hacker news comments. scores results by engagement. merges duplicates. then gives you 3-5 battle tested prompts with citations to where each one came from..

what's interesting is it also works for non prompt stuff. you can use it for

> research.. "/last30days what are traders saying about hyperliquid:native right now" pulls actual trader sentiment from x and reddit in one go

the crazy part imo is you're getting the wisdom of the last 30 days compressed into actionable output in under 5 minutes. 

manually doing this research takes me a full afternoon

heads up.. each query takes 2-8 minutes because it's actually scraping and synthesizing. not instant. some sources need api keys (tiktok needs scrapecreators). but the output is worth the wait

https://t.co/yOzzby2o0P

tweets.full_text:
Checked /cost at the start of my session this morning.

19,500 tokens before a single line of work.

Someone on Reddit audited 926 Claude Code sessions last week and found 45K at baseline.

Here is where it goes:

1) Every MCP server you have connected dumps its full tool definitions into context at session start, whether you call those tools or not.

2) CLAUDE.md loads in full on top of that, regardless of what the task actually needs.

3) Claude's own system tools take another 10-13k before you have typed a thing.

Two things that actually move it: 

Disable MCP servers you are not using for the current task + keep CLAUDE.md under 600 tokens.

Run /cost before you start your next session... it is the cheapest audit you will do this week.
